page: Microsoft Azure E8bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external block storage with Premium Storage compatibility.

Benchmark results place the E8bs_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The system achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1616.0 and a multi-core score of 6051.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 416.47 GB/sec, which drops to 57.57 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In database operations, the server processed 7.73 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 4132.17 on PassMark database benchmarks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks represent performance bottlenecks, falling into the bottom 25% of tested configurations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8bs_v5 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. The lack of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale Premium Storage independently. This configuration is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate web serving tasks. It is less suitable for compute-heavy parallel processing or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E8bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It lacks local storage and GPUs, instead supporting external Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks generally fall in the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6051.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.73 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and 7B LLM inference show weaker performance. This memory-optimized profile is suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and general enterprise applications requiring high memory density without excessive compute overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8bs_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and premium block storage capabilities for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capability with zero local storage bundled
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching benchmarks
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
